articles.title: Joe Mazzulla’s ‘tough love’ reminds Robert Parish of a particular Hall-of-Fame coach
articles.description:
Bill Fitch was the Celtics' coach when Parish arrived in Boston. "The Chief" sees similarities in Joe Mazzulla.

The post Joe Mazzulla’s ‘tough love’ reminds Robert Parish of a particular Hall-of-Fame coach appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Robert Parish compares Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla to Hall-of-Fame coach Bill Fitch.
Parish played under Fitch when he joined the Celtics in 1980.
Mazzulla's coaching style may influence team dynamics for Boston's NBA fans.

articles.title: Lukas Reichel has a tall task sparking dormant part of Bruins’ lineup
articles.description:

summary: Lukas Reichel joins the Bruins to energize a struggling lineup.

Boston fans expect increased competitiveness and excitement from the team.

articles.title: A Massachusetts police officer is running the Boston Marathon for veterans
articles.description:
On her third charity run, Laicey Ieronimo is supporting Semper Fi & America’s Fund and honoring the service of those closest to her.

The post A Massachusetts police officer is running the Boston Marathon for veterans appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Laicey Ieronimo, a Massachusetts police officer, is running the Boston Marathon for veterans.
This is Ieronimo's third charity run supporting Semper Fi & America's Fund.
Funds raised will directly benefit veterans in the Boston area.

articles.title: Cambridge Day founder Marc Levy is back with a new Cambridge and Somerville news hub
articles.description:
The mission: give locals a platform to stay informed and take part in civic conversation. As their tagline says, “We’re all in this together.”

articles.title: For this Boston Marathon runner, the starting line is everything
articles.description:
A Marine veteran and mental health professional is running to honor resilience — and support fellow service members.

The post For this Boston Marathon runner, the starting line is everything appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Marine veteran Gregory Baxter runs the Boston Marathon to honor resilience among service members.
Baxter, a mental health professional, aims to support fellow veterans on April 17, 2023.
His participation raises awareness for mental health issues affecting Boston's veteran community.

articles.title: Duke women’s basketball coach Kara Lawson needed a player to lead her team. She found that person in Dorchester’s Taina Mair.
articles.description:
Taina Mair was in a bit of slump — at least by her lofty standards — in the two weeks before the Atlantic Coast Conference tournament. Before Duke left for Duluth, Ga., coach Kara Lawson pulled Mair aside and gave her a dose of the honest feedback that defines their relationship. In order for the […]

The post Duke women’s basketball coach Kara Lawson needed a player to lead her team. She found that person in Dorchester’s Taina Mair. appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Duke coach Kara Lawson identified Dorchester's Taina Mair as a key team leader.
Mair experienced a performance slump before the ACC tournament, impacting team dynamics.
Mair's leadership could enhance Duke's chances in the NCAA tournament for Boston fans.

articles.title: Boston City Council to weigh non-citizen voting
articles.description:
A new home-rule petition would allow non-citizens with "legal status" to vote in municipal elections in Boston.

The post Boston City Council to weigh non-citizen voting appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Boston City Council considers allowing non-citizens with legal status to vote in local elections.
The home-rule petition aims to expand voting rights for approximately 30,000 non-citizens in Boston.
If approved, this change could significantly influence local policies and community representation.

articles.title: ‘He looks great’: Draymond Green had high praise for Jayson Tatum
articles.description:

summary: Draymond Green praised Jayson Tatum's performance during a recent game.

Local fans express excitement over Tatum's recovery and potential impact on the season.

articles.title: Two-time cancer survivor from Easton to run Boston Marathon for Mass General
articles.description:

summary: Kevin O’Rourke, a two-time cancer survivor, will run the Boston Marathon.

O’Rourke's story highlights the importance of local healthcare support for families.
